description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, test, release, deploy, int-test, and release-docker using the dazraf/build-tools:latest image (with docker:18.09-dind service) for .base_build jobs, a node:latest image for build:node-client, cordite/rtd:latest for documentation, and nimmaj/cordite-gcloud-alpine for deployment. Define a .base_build template that archives node/* and cordapps/build/gradle.log as artifacts, excludes specific branches/tags, and runs scripts to create build directories, execute Gradle builds (clean, checkLicense, build, deployNodes, buildNode, coveralls), then builds and pushes a Docker image; build:jar runs only on unprotected branches, build:sign:jar runs only on protected branches, both push the image, while build:node-client (allowed failure, except master/tags) runs npm install and build:pages runs make clean && make html. Deploy jobs deploy:edge and deploy:test extend .base_deploy, authenticate to GKE with a service‑account key, set the project/cluster, and execute upgradeGkeEnv.sh for the edge or test environment. Release jobs edge-release-docker, test-release-docker, release:gitlab-docker, release:maven, release:node-client, and release:pages push Docker images to the GitLab registry, tag and push to Docker Hub, publish Maven artifacts, npm publish, and upload documentation, each sending a Slack webhook notification; they run on master or version tags as specified. Integration test jobs int-test:edge and int-test:test (retry 2) run remote regression scripts, while test:integration and test:regression pull required images, run regression scripts, and use Docker‑in‑Docker with Kubernetes resource limits, all triggered by branch/tag rules and variable conditions.
